verses.commentary: Seemingly innocent action with tragic consequences.
Raped her (literally violated or humbled her). Brutal assault.
Ancient cultures often confused possession with love.
Get me this girl as wife.
Waiting for sons.
Hamor went to talk with Jacob.
Shocked and furious. The text calls it an outrage in Israel, using the new family name.
Please give her.
Intermarry with us. Broader proposal.
Settle among us. Land is open. Generous offer. But built on injustice.
Shechem tries to buy his way out.
I'll pay. Blank check offer. Desperation or arrogance?.
The text is clear: they're planning deception.
That would be a disgrace to us. Would be disgrace.
Become like us.
Take daughters. Settle. Become one people. False promise of unity.
We'll take our sister and go. Ultimatum.
Their proposal seemed good to Hamor and his son Shechem. They accept.
Shechem's eagerness leads to his doom.
Speak to men of city. Public assembly for decision.
Land has room. We can intermarry.
One condition. Males be circumcised.
They think they'll absorb Jacob's wealth.
All men agreed. City-wide covenant sign.
Dinah's full brothers (sons of Leah). Killed every male. Brutal revenge.
Left. Dinah rescued or taken? The text leaves her voice silent.
Sons of Jacob looted the city.
Everything. Total plunder. The violence escalates beyond justice.
They carried off all their wealth and all their women and children, taking as plunder everything in the houses. Everything.
Jacob's response. We are few. Jacob worries about consequences, not justice.
Jacob on his deathbed will curse this violence (49:5-7).
